reviews.review_description: Love this app - its absorbing, informative  and feels so immersive but I'm so disappointed that there have been no further additional areas. I have been looking forward to the promised Cordoba Mosque experience for quite a while now and would be grateful to know an approximate date? Carry on the wonderful work!

All in allt it pretty consistently feel like the tours captures just a tad bit to little of the area. I really liked the Japan scene but after a very engaging story about how important a restaurant was to the locals I was not able to enter it, quite a letdown. In Havanna you can see some side -streets on the map but you can't enter them, yet another slight frustration. The quality of the guides varies quite a bit but all in all they are pretty good.

The graphics is no better to me than the app. Wander' and you can go pretty much all over the world in it. However the items you can pick up are very sharp and clear like the silk shirt very realistic. I guess the technology isn't there yet to have a 360 HD view

developer_response.body: Hi Gary, we're sorry you didn't enjoy the experience. Keep in mind that this is not a 360º photo tour, Othersight is about exploring and moving through 3D spaces. We hope you enjoy it soon with the new locations, which will be released soon.

reviews.review_description:
This app offers very little.  You are then  sold any other experiences.
Garbage

developer_response.body:
Hello Artigort, we are sorry to hear that you did not like our experience.
The next spaces are going to be for free.  Thanks for your feedback.

reviews.review_title: OtherSide it's like a sci-fi Holodeck
reviews.review_description:
Wow... I've never seen anything like this in VR!
It's hard to imagine anything more realistic than the locations in this app (unless you enter a Start Trek holodeck).  The feeling of immersion is mind blowing. It is by far the best I have seen in Quest because the experience is so close to the reality that you are actually there; it's light years ahead of Brink in both realism and immersion. Also, touching the Tokyo shirt using hand tracking is quite an experience.
My congratulations to the team for reaching levels of realism never seen before in a Quest (I can't imagine how they have managed to make it work on Quest 1).
I can imagine a future travelling virtually to places of interest with applications like this. Amazing!
If they put in more locations - from what I've been able to read there's already one it - this app is a A+

It's kinda cool to be able to visit a museum or a church somewhere in the world without the need to get out of the house. The world of photogrammetry is pretty great to create things like these in VR (Also been working with it at university) and I know it's definitely not an easy job to get every texture in the correct way. Ofcourse you can see there are some rough edges that could have been done a bit better. But it all depends on the location I guess. 
The church for example looks awesome, the Havana street didn't really impress me...It did feel a bit empty. 
But the clothes.....wow. How the fabric moves and flows. Just impressing! Great job on that! 
I do miss haptics when you touch something. It would have been a nice extra. 
For now it's only 4 locations but each location has a guide that tells you about the places you visit, so it almost feels like a real trip to the museum. 
Hopefully we'll see some more locations added to the experience.... because for now it's kinda short. 
Looks great for what it is at the moment....but because of the lack in locations I'll give it 4* for now. 
Preferably I'd love to see some more indoor locations like other museums or something like the inside of a casa in Seville for example.

To the devs: thanks for addressing my loading issue, got it to start. ***BE SURE TO UPDATE YOUR QUEST***

Overall, an interesting addition to the VR your space.

Though the sound design is a little clunky, and the default locomotion is nauseating (highly recommend setting instant teleport as the default), the detail and unique interactions make the experiences worth a try.

Pricy for only four experiences but hopefully the library keeps growing. Nice work 👍

developer_response.body:
Hello! First of all, we are very sorry you had encounter that issue. 
We strongly recommend that you check if you are on the latest firmware version. 
Also, it is highly recommended that you have plenty of free memory, as low memory space often causes such problems.
If you still have this issue, please contact us at
info@raiznewmedia.com and we will try our best to fix your bad experience.

Best regards and thanks for trying OtherSight.

reviews.review_title: Closest thing to real travel in VR
reviews.review_description:
**Before you start, UPDATE YOUR QUEST. It won't work unless you update.**

Used this app for only a short while before quitting to write this review. If your favorite parts of travel are art and culture, you need this app. It feels as though you've wandered away from the big tourist areas to find those local surprises that make the trip.

The first location is like visiting a gallery with a private tour, but you can touch stuff, examine it, and not be arrested. Various objects also have audio info, and the graphics are astounding.

I love museums, and I'd swear I visited a new one today. After years of the pandemic, it's very welcome.

I've tried just the one location so far, but I can only assume they're all filled with as much careful detail.

This is a must-have for anyone who misses travel.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! We are happy that our intentions have encountered your wishes! We will add more spaces very soon, so stay tunned!

reviews.review_description:
It's not a game,  it's an experience.   It takes you to places you may never go in real life and gives you a history lesson of the locations as you walk freely around. 

I do think the price was a little high for the lack of content but if the add more locations,  it would even out.
When trying to download other locations, I get a no internet connection message, there is no problem with my connection to the Oculus. If no fix is forthcoming, will get a refund.

developer_response.body:
Hi Chris, we recommend to update to the last oculus OS version, and be sure to have enough space in your memory to allocate the new scenes.  Is the first time we receive this issue. Please contact us at info@raiznewmedia.com if the error persists. We would be pleased to help you.
Hope this solves your problem. Thanks for your feedback.

reviews.review_description: The visuals and the information are great there’s only a small handful of locations to try they definitely need to increase locations. It’s very cool to be able to go to locations that you’ve never been to before and learn about them

reviews.review_title: Short but sweet sad that there is no mor
reviews.review_description: There isn't lot and it's sad that there is no work done because so many potential but what there is  i love it so  just remember that its short but nice 4/5

reviews.review_title: Super Cool App with tons of content!
reviews.review_description:
This is super cool app with tons of content and different locations to choose from. Also you have plenty of things to hold and hear stories about them!

There is of course some texture that look not so sharp but it is not problem because this is all about experiencing these things!

You can explore it so many times and discover more things on your way!

Also everything is free even with upcoming updates comes new locations etc! 

I won't spoil too much but you have to just experience it yourself!

I like this app, well done. I can see this expanding and gaining popularity. 17.99 is totally worth it, I mean Ghostbusters afterlife.... 20 bucks..... Not worth it..... Taco Bell.  10 bucks  and you get the same shcitty food in a different wrapper. Not this app, it's different, it's a trend setter in my opinion. To the developers great job and what will your approach be with new content? Just curious. Have a good evening.
For the price, I want a richer experience
The controls are easy to use. I even looked through the photo album. I am glad that the captions were seen. I am hearing impaired.

reviews.review_title: I want a refund I dont know how to
reviews.review_description: This apps is not what I want  . This is unacceptable. Refund me . I don’t know how to proceed. But refund me .

After paying almost 20 bucks encouraged by all great reviews I read, I had great expectations, In a few words, my experience was like playing the app for firsts steps in oculus.
Maybe this was intended for educational purposes, but at last, I prefer watching free videos in oculus.
There are only 4 scenes availables, and you finish playing all in about 15 minutes.
Also you cannot change or disable language translation.

developer_response.body:
Hello Beckytuti, first of all, thanks for try OtherSight.

We are sorry we didnt fit your expectations. OtherSight is about exploring and presence. For that, multiplayer / Social mode is on the list for the near future update , and we will be adding more objects,  and new scenes will come very soon.
I encourage you to try navigating by hand-tracking system, that enforces the immersion, and makes it more natural and enchanting.

Also the voice-overs will be updated adding a mute option.

Thanks for your comment, we are working to improve the experience and keep you happy with your purchase. If you have any suggestion, please, extend your 
thoughts to info@raiznewmedia.com and we will be happy to receive your feedback. 

Greetings.

reviews.review_description:
👉webnetka*com 🇬🇧 🇫🇷 metaquest*forumactif*com👈
Edit: It is such a shame thzt it no longer evolves
Remains too limited: I remove a star
If you like virtual environments, if that's also part of what brought you to VR (traveling without moving from home, observing the world), then I guess you have to buy "OtherSight"
It is between a "Brink traveler" (many different sites but little text and interaction) and a "National Geographic Explore VR" (visit and explanations)
It is also completely urban and culture oriented compared to Brink which only shows natural landscapes (and NGeographic=between the 2)

You can walk around the sites (unlike Brink which is almost static), interact with objects, listen to information about the places.

In the 3 apps, the defect is that we always remain unsatisfied: we would really like more, but I suppose that if these applications with realistic photo environment do not flourish, it is because it remains expensive and/or long to produce (or niche market, in which case, you have every interest in encouraging these apps if you want to have more)

A little surprise on several sites of Othersite with fabrics that have a nice physics 

But in any case, these apps will have their little "wow" effect if shown in demo to people who don't know VR

if you want fun, games, or apps where you're going to spend hours, go your way
if you want to encourage these virtual travel apps or take advantage of their wow effect, then Othersite may be of interest to you
==================================
Pour échanger vos expériences et vos avis:
 👉 metaquest*forumactif*com 👈
Pour les promos quest : 👉 webnetka*com 👈
Good, but overpriced for too little content. Updates are promised but no dates
